description_long: This is a classic red fire hydrant with a cylindrical body and a domed top, featuring prominent vertical ridges and a weathered, chipped paint finish. The hydrant has three hose connection outlets: two large side outlets and one smaller front outlet, each with a circular cap. The top of the hydrant displays a central operating nut, surrounded by a series of raised ribs that extend down the cap. The surface is predominantly red with visible white scuff marks and some blackened areas, suggesting exposure to urban elements and frequent use. The base is thick and sturdy, designed to be bolted to the ground for stability. No chains or manufacturer markings are visible, and the hydrant appears robust and functional, suitable for emergency use in city environments.
description: A weathered red fire hydrant with three hose outlets, a domed top, and visible wear marks. It features a sturdy cylindrical body and prominent caps for hose connections.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main outlet with a circular cap and detailed vertical ridges.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting one of the large hose outlets and the domed top.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the cylindrical body with vertical ridges and no visible outlets.
description_view_3: Angled view showing a side outlet and the main front outlet together.
